    "id": "zplugin000",
    "type": "component",
    "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
    "applicationVersion": {
      "version": "0.0.1"
    },
    "title": "{{appTitle}}",
    "description": "{{appDescription}}",
    "resources": "resources.json",
    "sourceTemplate": {
      "id": "@sap/generator-fiori:basic",
      "version": "1.20.2",
      "toolsId": "5f79ee28-1af6-428c-84a7-62830ac66232"
    },
    "crossNavigation": {
      "inbounds": {
        "Shell-plugin": {
          "semanticObject": "Shell",
          "action": "plugin",
          "hideLauncher": true,
          "signature": {
            "parameters": {},
            "additionalParameters": "allowed"
          }
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "sap.ui": {
    "technology": "UI5",
    "icons": {
      "icon": "",
      "favIcon": "",
      "phone": "",
      "phone@2": "",
      "tablet": "",
      "tablet@2": ""
    },
    "deviceTypes": {
      "desktop": true,
      "tablet": true,
      "phone": true
    }
  },
  "sap.ui5": {
    "flexEnabled": true,
    "dependencies": {
      "minUI5Version": "1.144.1",
      "libs": {
        "sap.m": {},
        "sap.ui.core": {}
      }
    },
    "contentDensities": {
      "compact": true,
      "cozy": true
    },
    "models": {
      "i18n": {
        "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
        "settings": {
          "bundleName": "zplugin000.i18n.i18n"
        }
      }
    },
    "resources": {
      "css": [
        {
          "uri": "css/style.css"
        }
      ]
    }
  },
  "sap.cloud": {
    "public": true,
    "service": "zplugin000"
  },
  "sap.flp": {
    "type": "plugin"
  }
}

Component.js :- 

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/core/UIComponent",
    "zplugin000/model/models",
    "sap/ushell/Container",
    "sap/m/Text",
], (UIComponent, models, Container, Text) => {
    "use strict";

    return UIComponent.extend("zplugin000.Component", {
        metadata: {
            manifest: "json",
            interfaces: [
                "sap.ui.core.IAsyncContentCreation"
            ]
        },

        init() {
            // call the base component's init function
            UIComponent.prototype.init.apply(this, arguments);

            // set the device model
            this.setModel(models.createDeviceModel(), "device");

            // enable routing
            this.getRouter().initialize();

            // enable footer
            this.setupFooter();
        },
        setupFooter: async function () {
            const FrameBoundExtension = await Container.getServiceAsync("FrameBoundExtension");
            let nCurrentYear = new Date().getFullYear();
            await FrameBoundExtension.createFooter({
                id: "flpFooter",
                contentLeft: [
                    new Text({
                        id: "copyrightStatement",
                        text: this.getModel("i18n").getResourceBundle().getText("copyrightStatement", [nCurrentYear])
                    })
                ]
            });
        },
    });
});

SAPUI5  #Shell Plugin SAP Fiori Makers

entry.title: Not Getting Email Notifications for Comments Posted on my Blog Posts
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Since some time, it is noted that I am not getting Emails for the Comments posted on my Blog Post. This is so when is no notification settings change (Email for Comments on my subscribed items still remains set) and when I am already subscribed to my post.

This was observed by my other colleague also. Tried changing the settings but no luck. Example post - Managing Invoices with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

Kindly check on this. Missing to reply on important comments.

 - Abhijit

entry.title: Transpose Characteristics Values from Rows to Columns in Efficient Way
entry.content:
Hello,

Currently, for Batch classification, we have around 200 characteristics, which are defined as single values.

In order to use them in reports and other functionalities, we had created two CDS Views.
First one, reads data from database table AUSP and makes associations to database tables CABN, CAWN and CAWNT. 
Second one, reads data from first one, group it by Classification Object Key and with use of statement 'CASE' + 'MAX', transposes them to columns, with use of Characteristic Name.

Functionality works as desired, but is not very efficient.

As a proof of concept, I had created Table Function, which is doing the same operations, but unfortunately, it was slightly slower than those CDSes.

Different option would be to create database table, where columns represent characteristic values, and are populated / updated, while Batch Classification is saved. 
Then, instead of transposing data from AUSP, we could use data, which is already transposed.

Do You have any other ideas, of how to make it in an efficient way?

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content:
本文内容来自SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs - SAP Community

 

动机：

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 已经到来，它是数据和分析的未来。BDC于2025年2月13日发布，代表着SAP数据和分析解决方案的重大演进。我们理解您渴望了解更多关于这个改变游戏规则的平台，您可能也有很多问题。本常见问题解答旨在为最常见的疑问提供清晰、简洁的答案，让您对BDC有扎实的理解。它补充了Business Unleashed活动、SAP帮助文档以及我们产品同事专家博客中分享的丰富信息。SAP Business Data Cloud于2025年2月13日发布，将以受控方式商业化，计划于2025年4月全面上市（可能有变动）。让我们开始吧！

免责声明：

本博客中分享的日期和信息反映了我撰写时对SAP的最佳理解，可能会随着SAP产品、功能和时间表的变化而变化。更新于2025年12月4日。

SAP Business Data Cloud组件：

 

常见问题与解答（FAQ）：

我们按SAP BDC组件对问题进行了分组，以使信息清晰、简单且针对特定组件。

SAP Business Data Cloud：

1. 什么是SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)？

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 是一个改变游戏规则的平台，为您的所有数据和分析提供单一、完全托管的云SaaS平台。它汇集所有SAP数据，集成第三方数据源，并提供强大的治理功能。BDC提供丰富的数据产品、高级分析、交叉分析、基准数据和AI驱动的智能应用程序，使您能够做出更明智的决策并改善业务成果。它为SAP ERP S/4HANA（PCE、公有云）和业务线应用程序（如HCM、ISBN等）提供可操作的洞察，以推动更有影响力的决策。该平台实现了无缝的增量数据湖仓架构，结合了SAP Datasphere、SAP Analytics Cloud、SAP BW或BW/4HANA PCE（私有云版本）、托管SAP Databricks、解决方案扩展SAP Snowflake、SAP BDC Connect和智能应用程序的功能。它提供带有数据产品的业务特定数据包，支持BI、ML、AI服务、Joule、知识图谱和规划功能。

了解更多：https://www.sap.com/products/data-cloud.html

2. SAP BDC的关键组件和构建模块是什么？

  • 基础服务和数据产品
  • 智能应用程序（原洞察应用）
  • BDC驾驶舱
  • SAP Datasphere
  • SAP Analytics Cloud
  • SAP BW、BW/4HANA PCE（私有云版本）
  • SAP Databricks
  • SAP BDC Connect
  • SAP Snowflake（计划2026年第一季度全面上市）

3. 是什么驱动SAP将SAP Datasphere、SAP SAC和SAP BW、BW4整合到新的SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)中？

虽然SAP Datasphere业务数据结构架构是积极的举措，但它并未完全实现真正统一的语义数据平台的目标。SAP BDC通过以下方式解决这些局限性：

  • 为所有数据和分析需求提供一个软件即服务(SaaS)平台和订阅模型
  • 统一数据体验：通过整合这些解决方案，SAP旨在提供无缝的数据体验，使客户能够跨不同SAP应用程序和第三方数据源访问、管理和分析数据
  • 自动化数据集成：BDC提供SAP托管的数据集成，包括预构建的数据产品和智能应用程序，无需客户手动集成数据，即使是来自SAP数据源的数据
  • 通过AI/ML扩展分析：它集成了SAP Databricks，扩展分析能力并支持全面的ML/AI用例
  • 提供具有成本效益的存储、计算和完全启用的数据湖仓架构
  • 提供SAP托管的内容：如智能应用程序，与传统业务内容相比简化了部署

4. 与其他数据和分析解决方案相比，SAP Business Data Cloud的关键差异化因素是什么，这些差异如何转化为客户的实际利益？

SAP Business Data Cloud为客户提供跨SAP ERP和业务线应用程序的统一数据基础。它通过在这些系统中集成单一、一致的数据模型来实现这一目标，支持强大的跨职能分析、规划以及AI/ML的应用。本质上，它是一套完全托管的服务套件，涵盖从构建现代业务数据结构和数据仓库到处理规划、数据工程和AI/ML工作负载的所有内容。

SAP Business Data Cloud为客户带来的好处：

  • 开箱即用的洞察和报告：预构建的、SAP托管的洞察应用程序提供跨所有业务线的高级分析和规划能力，提供推动更好决策的可操作洞察
  • 即用型数据产品：关键业务流程的预构建和SAP托管数据产品保持基本的业务背景和语义，同时确保全面的数据治理。通过提供随时可用的高质量数据，加快实现价值的时间
  • 具有高级数据仓库的统一数据环境：它协调SAP和第三方数据，简化数据集成和管理。业务数据结构架构、业务仓库现代化、规划、数据工程和AI/ML的完全托管能力进一步简化操作并降低复杂性
  • ML和AI：SAP Databricks集成允许AI、机器学习和数据工程任务的专业代码开发，满足数据科学家和开发人员的需求
  • 可扩展且高效的基础：基于增量数据湖仓架构构建，利用HANA Data Lake Files和Apache Spark计算，该平台可以高效处理海量数据，确保随着数据增长的性能和可扩展性

简而言之，这是满足所有数据和分析需求的一个SaaS解决方案。它涵盖BI分析（交叉）、规划、AI。SAP Business Data Cloud简化数据管理，加速洞察，并使企业能够充分利用其数据的潜力，改善运营、规划和创新。

5. SAP Business Data Cloud中的基础服务是什么，它们做什么？

基础服务是支持数据产品和智能应用程序的基本后台组件。它们处理从系统设置和获取数据，到转换数据、构建数据产品和保持系统平稳运行的所有事务。它们使用开放资源发现(ORD)从不同来源查找数据产品。数据存储在HANA Data Lake Files (HDLF)即Delta lake表（一种快速、高效的文件格式）中，通过SQL on file、Delta Sharing开源协议访问。Delta Sharing原则是一种无需复制即可共享数据的方式，使其能够以安全、可扩展的方式被许多不同工具访问。本质上，基础服务是使您的ERP、LoB数据作为精心策划的数据产品随时可用并在BDC中使用的引擎。

6. SAP Business Data Cloud中的数据产品是什么，有哪些类型，如何创建？

SAP Business Data Cloud中的数据产品是一个独立的、业务就绪的数据集，设计用于轻松使用。它不仅仅是原始数据；它包括必要的业务背景和语义含义，确保用户理解数据代表什么。关键的是，数据产品得到完全治理，并通过API和数据产品目录提供，使其易于在整个组织中发现和使用。数据包基本上将相关的数据产品分组。您可以从BDC驾驶舱激活数据包，存在三种类型的数据产品：

  • 精心策划的、SAP托管的数据产品：这些由SAP预构建，最初专注于来自ERP和业务线应用程序的数据。它们遵循单一域模型，使用标准类型系统以保持一致性
  • 客户定义的数据产品：这些由客户创建以满足其特定的分析需求，利用各种来源的数据
  • 合作伙伴定义的数据产品：这些由SAP合作伙伴开发，提供针对特定行业或用例的专业数据集

6.1 SAP托管和客户托管数据产品之间有什么区别？

SAP托管数据产品：

  • 这些在整个生命周期中由SAP完全管理
  • 数据存储在基础服务(FOS) HDLFS中，客户无法直接访问

客户托管数据产品：

  • 这些由客户端到端管理
  • 数据存储在SAP Datasphere对象存储中，该存储基于HDLFS构建

7. BDC数据产品和SAP Datasphere市场是一回事吗？

不，它们是不同的概念/产品。BDC数据产品和SAP Datasphere市场服务于不同的目的。虽然两者都涉及数据产品的概念，但市场充当购买第三方数据、数据集货币化以及促进组织内部或与合作伙伴之间数据共享的平台。相反，BDC数据产品主要专注于提供语义丰富的业务应用程序数据。

8. SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 如何存储其数据？

使用SAP HANA Data Lake Files (HDLF) 作为底层存储机制来存储数据。数据存储在Delta表中，这是Delta Lake架构的一部分。这种设置使BDC能够高效管理大量数据，具有ACID事务、可扩展的元数据处理以及统一的流式和批量数据处理等特性。这种方法具有成本效益且可扩展，通过HDFL、Apache Spark计算和文件上的SQL支持多TB级存储和处理。数据使用奖章架构（青铜、白银和黄金表）进行组织，以提供随时可用的数据产品。

9. SAP BDC中的智能应用程序是什么？

智能应用程序是SAP BDC中预构建的分析应用程序。它们帮助您快速发现隐藏的洞察并做出更快的决策。它们由SAP完全托管，基于精心策划的BDC数据产品、Datasphere模型、SAC故事构建，包括预定义的指标、AI模型和规划工具。

10. SAP Business Data Cloud中的智能应用程序与SAP S/4HANA、Ariba、SuccessFactors等中的嵌入式分析有何不同，这些应用程序将如何构建？

答：智能应用程序服务于不同的分析和AI/ML驱动的用例，专注于历史和跨应用程序数据以进行更深入的分析，不同于强调实时、日常操作数据的嵌入式运营报告。嵌入式分析通常难以处理高数据密集型分析用例或跨多个系统整合数据，这正是智能应用程序要解决的挑战。

11. BDC智能应用程序与SAP预构建业务内容有什么区别，特别是考虑到预构建业务内容通常是免费的？客户能否只定制预构建业务内容而不购买智能应用程序？

智能应用程序与业务内容之间的根本区别在于生命周期管理。它们由SAP完全托管（一键安装并随时可用），包括数据集成、更新和持续支持。智能应用程序通常不是免费的，需要订阅或购买。另一方面，SAP业务内容通常无需额外费用，但将数据集成、配置、更新和维护的责任转移给客户（导入、部署、数据集成、更新等）。

12. 现有的SAP S/4HANA嵌入式分析或业务线应用程序（如Ariba）中的嵌入式SAP Analytics Cloud会有任何变化吗？

不，不会有任何变化。

13. 智能应用程序与SAP Datasphere和SAC的业务内容有何不同？

业务内容是一个包含源连接、数据模型（视图）和故事的包，客户必须自己设置和使用，而连接数据并不总是容易的。智能应用程序开箱即用。当您安装智能应用程序时，数据、分析和应用程序都已为您设置和配置好。此外，智能应用程序使用预构建的、SAP托管的数据产品，您也可以使用这些产品更快地创建自己的洞察和构建AI应用程序。

14. SAP Business Data Cloud智能应用程序何时可用于特定的业务线(LoB)应用程序，如Ariba、Concur和SuccessFactors等？

长期目标是为所有业务和LoB应用程序提供智能应用程序。然而，这将逐步推出，并将发布详细的路线图。虽然SAP优先为核心业务功能构建应用程序，但他们也将与合作伙伴合作创建特定行业的解决方案。可用性的更新将尽快分享。

15. 客户能否定制或增强SAP Business Data Cloud中的预构建智能应用程序？

是的，客户可以在SAP Datasphere和SAP Analytics Cloud中扩展智能应用程序以满足其特定需求。但是，这些定制将由客户管理，因为它们将基于SAP托管内容的副本。

16. SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 如何访问其他SAP和非SAP应用程序的数据？

SAP BDC利用SAP Datasphere的原生数据集成能力，包括第三方连接（数据流、复制流、远程表等），还利用Databricks向基础数据层的回写功能。此外，客户可以利用SAP Datasphere嵌入式对象存储进行数据访问和存储。

17. Business Data Cloud驾驶舱是什么？

驾驶舱是Business Data Cloud中的一个新界面。您可以在这里处理管理任务，如安装数据包和智能应用程序。

18. 如何订阅SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)，定价如何？

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 通过SaaS订阅提供，使用BDC容量单位(CU)定价。定价结构有两个主要组成部分：

BDC核心容量：此组件涵盖SAP Business Data Cloud中的四项主要技术服务。容量单位(CU)充当灵活的令牌，允许您根据需要在这4项核心服务中分配它们。例如，客户1可能选择Datasphere、SAC和Databricks的组合，客户2可能选择Datasphere、SAC和BW/4HANA PCE，而客户3可能只选择SAC。这种灵活的令牌分配确保您只使用所需的服务。

  • SAP Datasphere服务
  • SAP Analytics Cloud服务
  • SAP BW和BW/4HANA私有云版本(PCE)服务
  • SAP Databricks服务
  • SAP BDC connect
  • SAP Snowflake

智能应用程序：此组件涵盖预构建的、特定业务线(LoB)的应用程序，包括SAP托管的数据产品、语义模型和仪表板。客户必须至少许可一个洞察应用。

有关详细定价信息以及根据您的特定需求定制订阅，请联系您指定的SAP客户经理(AE)或客户成功合作伙伴/客户成功经理(CSP/CSM)。

19. 如何计算或估算SAP BDC和组件的大小？

带我去SAP BDC估算器。SAP Databricks定价计算器。

20. 如果客户想要构建三层环境，配置如何工作？他们能否在这些层之间分配总容量单位(CU)？

是的，客户将能够配置SAP Datasphere、SAP Analytics Cloud、SAP BW/4HANA私有云版本(PCE)、SAP Databricks的多个租户，并相应地分配其CU。

21. 在哪里可以找到SAP BDC路线图？

SAP BDC路线图

22. 已经有Datasphere的合同（BTPEA/CPEA）。这与SAP BDC的不同定价如何协调？我可以使用现有合同试用SAP BDC吗？

现有合同将继续有效，不会出现问题，并且在商业和技术上都将与SAP BDC无缝集成。但是，SAP BDC使用单独的订阅模式，因此您的现有合同不会直接覆盖SAP BDC的使用。您可以通过rewire方式将现有合同转移到SAP BDC。

23. 我现在可以订阅SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)吗？

是的，BDC目前可供订阅。如有任何疑问或想了解订阅选项，请联系您的SAP代表。

24. SAP BDC在公有云和私有云或NS2或主权云中都可用吗？

SAP Business Data Cloud目前仅作为公有云SaaS解决方案提供。NS2或主权云产品暂定于2026年推出。

25. 如果SAP Business Data Cloud数据中心在我所在的地区尚未可用怎么办？

如果BDC数据中心不在您的地区，您有两个选择：1) 您仍然可以购买BDC并使用另一个地区的数据中心，或者 2) 您可以继续使用SAP Datasphere和SAP Analytics Cloud，直到BDC在本地可用。这样，每个人都可以获得合适的解决方案。

26. SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)支持哪些SAP S/4HANA版本，是否计划将支持扩展到本地部署版本？

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 战略性地支持SAP S/4HANA的云版本，具体包括：

  • SAP S/4HANA私有云版本：专门提供SAP托管数据产品
  • SAP S/4HANA公有云版本：专门提供SAP托管数据产品

虽然SAP托管数据产品专门针对这些云版本提供，但目前没有将支持扩展到本地部署S/4HANA系统的即时计划。但是，SAP将为SAP BDC提供客户托管选项，使SAP S/4HANA本地部署客户能够连接其系统并将数据集成到BDC中，以获得全面的数据和分析解决方案。

27. 长期战略是否是将当前存储在单独应用程序（SAP S/4HANA、SuccessFactors、Ariba等）中的所有数据都放在SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)中，而应用程序更多地作为访问数据的服务，而无需单独存储数据？

不适用于ERP或业务应用程序中的事务数据。SAP BDC主要用于分析、规划和AI用例，支持跨SAP和非SAP应用程序使用数据。它将带有语义的业务数据移动到BDC层，这将集中数据管理，满足所有数据需求，同时从核心事务或业务应用程序中卸载存储。

28. SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)是否提供试用或免费层租户访问？我可以使用现有的BTPEA合同获得SAP Datasphere和SAC等组件的免费层体验吗？

SAP Business Data Cloud基础试用。在此自助注册。

  • 时间限制：30天
  • 可用服务：BDC核心（SAC、Datasphere、SAP Databricks）、营运资金智能应用程序、数据产品

SAP Datasphere和SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)是BDC的关键组件，确实为现有BTP客户提供免费层选项。这些客户可以访问这些服务，只要这些服务在BTP服务目录中可用，就可以探索SAP Datasphere和SAC功能。Datasphere和SAC计划从新BTPEA中移除...

29. SAP BDC支持或将支持哪些超大规模云服务提供商？

AWS、GCP（2025年7月）和Azure（2025年8月和2026年4月）。

30. Insight Apps和智能应用程序之间有什么区别？Insight Apps怎么了？

从高层次来看，这主要是品牌重塑。Insight Apps已更名为智能应用程序，以与下一代开箱即用的解决方案保持一致，这些解决方案将由AI驱动并与Joule集成。这些应用程序通过嵌入式和融合式AI功能变得更加智能。

 

 

 

SAP Datasphere 

1. SAP BDC 如何影响现有的 SAP Datasphere 和 SAC 客户，过渡过程是怎样的？重新连接流程如何运作？

现有的 Datasphere 和 SAC 客户可以继续按原样使用他们的解决方案，但这些解决方案将不被视为 BDC 框架的一部分。或者，他们可以"重新连接"其租户以与 BDC 集成。这种过渡被设计为无缝的，不会中断当前运营或现有 API。虽然目前 BDC 需要单独的 Datasphere 租户，但 SAP 正在探索自动升级的选项。最终，Datasphere 和 SAC 将成为 BDC 框架的集成组件。没有立即切换的压力，让客户在采用时间表上保持灵活性。

重新连接流程步骤：将独立的 SAP Datasphere 和 SAP Analytics Cloud 订阅重新连接到 SAP Business Data Cloud...

重新连接现有 SAP Datasphere 或 SAC 租户的过程涉及更改租户的元数据，将租户分配到 SAP BDC 架构下。客户在重新连接完成后不会经历任何系统停机或租户中的可见变化。该过程仅更新内部租户元数据（SAP 用于识别），不会影响客户的配置设置（如端口或 IP 地址）、资产或数据。重新连接是就地执行的，因此不需要租户迁移或替换。对于 SAP 管理的订阅租户（如 SAC 或 Datasphere），重新连接将在合同开始/续订日期由 SAP 通过重组合同自动处理。对于客户管理的租户（基于 BTP），客户必须在购买 BDC 订阅后手动启动重新连接。

更新：通过 SAP Business Data Cloud 提供 SAP Datasphere 和 SAP Analytics Cloud

2. SAP Datasphere 在 BDC 中的未来是什么，它会继续开发吗？

SAP Datasphere 仍然是 BDC 的战略组件，将继续得到增强。BDC 中的 Datasphere 与独立的 Datasphere 之间不会有功能差距，它将保持自己的路线图。

3. 随着 SAP BDC 的推出，SAP Datasphere 将如何变化？

在 Datasphere 层面，没有技术变化。实际上，Datasphere 将受益于 SAP 精选的 BDC 数据产品、智能应用程序、零复制集成以及与 Databricks 的 Delta Sharing。您可以继续在这些可用数据产品之上构建自定义模型。

4. 如果我需要的数据在 BDC 中不作为数据产品提供怎么办？Datasphere 中的原生连接是否相关？

SAP Datasphere 提供了广泛的内置连接，可连接到各种数据源，无论它们是本地还是云端，或来自 SAP 或非 SAP 系统。如果数据不是作为数据产品、Insight 应用程序或第三方解决方案现成提供的，您可以使用这些连接将数据导入 BDC。这允许您利用来自 SAP 和非 SAP 源的数据来构建您的分析用例。您可以在各种场景中使用原生集成，例如：

集成不作为数据产品提供的标准 CDS 视图。 集成根据您特定需求定制的自定义 CDS 视图。 使用现有的数据集成功能连接到 SAP 源。 将来自第三方（非 SAP）源的数据与您的 SAP 数据一起集成。

5. SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 如何处理数据集成，特别是在混合 HANA 建模场景中，客户需要考虑哪些关键因素？

BDC 采用类似于 Databricks 的零复制数据集成方法，以最小化数据冗余并提高效率。对于自定义数据产品，数据必须驻留在 SAP Datasphere 中，后者作为中央数据集成和建模中心。BDC 正在积极改进与非 SAP 系统的连接，并鼓励客户反馈。

关于混合 HANA 建模场景，客户应利用 SAP Datasphere 的自定义数据集成功能。Datasphere 正在投资 HANA 工件的语义导入，使客户能够直接在 BDC 中利用现有的 HANA 开发。这种方法简化了集成，并最大化了 HANA 模型在 BDC 环境中的可用性。

6. BDC 中的报告如何工作，与独立 Datasphere 相比有什么区别？

报告工具，特别是 SAC，允许通过与 Datasphere 的实时连接立即进行报告。一个关键区别是在 BDC 中增加了 Joule。

7. SAP Datasphere 是否包含在 BDC 中，我是否需要单独的许可证？定价如何运作？

是的，Datasphere 是 BDC 的关键组件。您将获得足够的 BDC 容量许可来满足您的 Datasphere 需求，类似于您今天的做法，使用即将推出的新 BDC 评估工具。Datasphere 本身的定价将保持不变。

8. SAP Datasphere 和 SAC 上的业务内容现在仍然可用吗（既然我们有了智能应用程序），我们应该何时建议使用其中一个而不是另一个？

智能应用程序是一项重大进步，提供更全面、更高效的分析体验。这是由 SAP 管理的端到端生命周期。它们通过将流行的业务内容转换为一键安装的应用程序来简化部署，自动化数据集成、数据加载、数据模型和仪表板的设置。此外，BDC 管理端到端的数据提取和暂存，建立通过 Delta Sharing 协议访问的零复制数据基础。这使得智能应用程序成为首选，提供比手动管理预构建内容更简单、更自动化和更强大的解决方案。

9. SAP Datasphere 对象存储和 BDC 基础存储层是相同的吗？

不，它们是不同的。虽然两者都是基于 HANA Data Lake Files 的对象存储，但 BDC 基础存储层是 SAP 管理的集中式数据湖仓，作为整个 BDC 平台的核心存储库。它支持智能应用程序等功能并确保统一的数据访问。相比之下，SAP Datasphere 对象存储是 Datasphere 中的一个组件，为客户提供在 Datasphere 环境中存储和管理数据的灵活性。它通常用于暂存、数据准备和特定用例。

一个关键区别是访问权限：SAP BDC 基础层由 SAP 管理，客户无法访问。但是，客户保留对 SAP Datasphere 对象存储中数据的控制权。在这里，他们可以通过将数据复制到文件空间来构建自定义数据产品，并根据需要创建分层架构（如青铜层、白银层、黄金层），通过使用 Apache Spark 运行时的转换流执行转换，然后将其共享到标准空间以用于流、视图和分析模型。

 

 

 

SAP Analytics Cloud:

1. SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) 在 SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 中扮演什么角色？

SAC 是 BDC 的组成部分，与 SAP Datasphere 和 Business Warehouse 一起提供统一的体验。这种组合产品能够在所有业务线中提供全面的洞察。现有 SAC 客户可以继续使用其当前合同或无缝集成地过渡到 BDC。SAC Enterprise 也将继续作为独立产品提供。

2. SAP Business Data Cloud 是否会取代 SAP Analytics Cloud？

不，BDC 不会取代 SAC。相反，它是 SAP 数据、规划和分析解决方案的演进，将 Datasphere、SAC 和 BW 整合在一起，提供更统一和强大的体验。SAC 可以作为 BDC 内容（如智能应用程序）的展示和消费层。

3. 当 SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) 成为 SAP BDC 的一部分时，与我们今天的使用方式相比，是否会有任何限制？

不，在功能或特性方面不会有差异，也没有限制。您可以像今天一样继续使用 SAC。但是，如果使用具有 SAC 故事板作为仪表板的开箱即用智能应用程序，您将获得好处。

4. SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) 在 SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 中的许可如何运作？

虽然 SAC 将包含在 BDC 中并计入整体 BDC 容量单位成本，但其许可仍将基于 BI 和规划许可证的数量，并提供转换工具。现有 SAC 和 Datasphere 客户可以继续使用其当前许可证而不会中断。与 BDC 的集成将是无缝的。

5. BDC 将如何影响我们当前的 SAP Webi/Business Objects 环境？

BDC 不会直接影响您现有的 Webi/Business Objects 环境。但是，要利用 Business Data Cloud 中的功能，您需要过渡到 SAP Analytics Cloud。

6. SAC 嵌入式版本是否在 BDC 中？规划功能是否仅限于独立的 SAC Enterprise 版本？

不，SAC 嵌入式版本不是 BDC 的一部分；它仍然保留在业务线解决方案中。但是，规划功能将作为 BDC 中 SAC 的一部分提供（与 Datasphere 的无缝规划）。

 

 

 

SAP BW、BW/4HANA PCE（私有云版本）和 SAP Datasphere、BW bridge：

1. 随着 SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 的推出，我的本地 SAP Business Warehouse (BW 或 BW/4HANA) 会发生什么，为什么我应该考虑升级/迁移？

SAP BDC 代表了本地 BW 系统的未来。通过迁移到 BDC 中 BW 的私有云版本，您可以保护现有数据和投资，同时获得增强的功能。您将保留熟悉的 BW 功能并解锁新的可能性，例如将 BW 数据转换为数据产品，实现与其他系统的无缝数据共享，并利用 BDC 与 Databricks 集成的数据工程、数据科学和 AI/ML 工具。这种迁移允许您在现代数据架构中公开传统 BW Info-Provider 数据，快速协调传统和新数据以改进洞察，为 AI/ML 用例扩展 BW 数据，并按照自己的节奏过渡到云端。基本上是从客户管理的数据仓库转移到 SAP 管理的数据仓库解决方案，包含数据产品和智能应用程序。

2. 在 BDC 中拥有 SAP BW[BW/4] 有什么好处，为什么我应该从 SAP BW 7.5 迁移到 BDC 中的 BW 7.5 PCE 或从 BW/4HANA 迁移到 BW/4HANA PCE？

集成到 BDC 提供了显著的优势。具体来说，SAP BW 客户可以将其系统提升到 SAP 管理的 PCE 环境，无需立即进行复杂的迁移，受益于扩展支持，并允许将 BW 用例逐步过渡到 SAP BDC，例如用 Datasphere 的功能替换数据流，节省时间和资源。（注意：BW 7.5 必须在 HANA DB 上才能与 BDC 集成。）

战略优势： 与 SAP BDC 完全一致。 遵守清洁核心原则。 BW 7.5 PCE 主流维护至 2030 年，BW/4HANA PCE 至 2040 年。 采用 SaaS 商业模式，使用 CU 指标。

技术优势：

  • 允许代码增强和修改。
  • 可扩展性和 SAP 管理的技术操作，应客户要求进行升级。
  • 为 BW Infoproviders（包括查询）、Multi providers、aDSO、Info Objects 提供开箱即用的数据产品生成器，并结合 BW 传统数据与新数据。
  • 所有 BW 对象（数据产品）可通过 BDC 目录功能访问。
  • 将 BW 数据附加到数据架构以启用 BI、AI/ML 用例。
  • Delta Sharing（零复制）实现与 SAP Databricks 的数据共享。
  • 直接在 SAP Datasphere 分析层中消费。

3. 谁负责将本地 SAP BW、BW4 系统提升到 SAP BDC PCE，SAP 的提升和转移服务的相关成本是多少？可能需要多长时间？

在高层次上，该过程涉及协作努力。SAP 企业云服务 (ECS) 团队将管理底层基础设施及其配置，BW 系统本身的实际提升和转移通常由 SAP 服务或 RISE 认证合作伙伴执行。目前客户无法自行执行迁移。

关于成本，提升和转移以及 ECS 基础设施配置确实涉及服务费用。但是，这些可能是有吸引力的产品的一部分。请与您的 SAP 客户经理 (AE)、客户成功合作伙伴 (CSP) 或客户成功经理 (CSM) 联系，以获取详细信息并探索潜在选项。

提升和转移的时间范围可能在 2 到 3 个月之间，根据客户的 BW 环境而有所不同。

4. 一旦我们的 SAP BW 或 BW/4HANA 系统迁移到 SAP BDC PCE，有什么好处，过渡过程是怎样的？

迁移到 BDC 中的 BW PCE 或 BW/4HANA PCE 后，您可以获得几个关键好处和简化的过渡：

开箱即用的数据产品生成：PCE 版本包含一个数据产品生成器，可无缝将 BW 对象作为数据产品发布到 SAP Datasphere 对象存储中。 统一数据基础：您可以利用现有的 BW 数据与 SAP 和非 SAP 数据一起，创建强大而统一的数据基础。 目录集成：您的 BW 对象将在 BDC 目录功能中随时可用，增强可发现性和可访问性。 逐步 Datasphere 过渡：您可以逐步将 BW 数据流迁移到 SAP Datasphere，允许分阶段和受控的过渡。

5. 将 BW 本地系统现代化为 SAP BDC 中的 BW PCE 的客户用例是什么？

主要用例包括：

协调数据：在 SAP Datasphere 中将 SAP BW 数据与 SAP BDC 数据产品集成，使用现代的基于 SQL 的建模方法将传统数据和新数据整合在一起。 启用 AI/ML：通过利用有价值的 BW 数据，为机器学习和人工智能计划提供坚实的基础。 一个统一的数据平台：创建一个通用数据平台，以满足组织内不同的数据消费需求。

6. 客户可以保留其本地 SAP BW 并连接到 BDC 而不提升到 PCE 吗？

不可以，客户必须迁移到 BDC 中的私有云版本 (PCE) 才能充分利用其好处。否则，他们将无法访问 SAP 管理的数据产品、智能应用程序，或使用数据产品生成器生成 BW 数据产品的能力。

7. 现有的 SAP BW/4HANA 私有云版本 (PCE) 客户在 SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 中会发生什么？

如果您已经拥有 SAP BW/4HANA PCE，您可以将其连接到 BDC。您不必立即切换到 BDC，但当您的合同到期续约时，您可以选择将 BDC 作为交易的一部分。如果您使用 BW/4HANA 的私有定制选项 (PTO)，您可以连接到 BDC，但目前还不能在商业上切换。

8. SAP Datasphere、BW bridge 组件的未来是什么？

SAP Datasphere BW bridge 可选组件仍然相关，并支持至少到 2040 年的新项目。它服务于两个主要目的或用例：

访问 BW 内容和 ECC 提取器：它能够访问 SAP BW 业务内容和 ECC 提取器，包括 SAPI 和客户生成的提取器。 BW 系统退役：它通过允许将 BW 资产重用和迁移到 SAP Datasphere（有一定限制）来促进 BW 系统的退役。这允许它作为数据摄取和暂存层运行，保留大部分 BW 建模功能，但不包括 BW 查询。

注意：虽然除了解决一些缺失的功能（如从 Datasphere 导入 BW 模型）之外，没有计划重大的新功能，但它提供了与 Datasphere 集成的途径。它不在 SAP BDC 创新路径上。对于未使用 BW Bridge 的现有 BW 客户，SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 提供了到 BW 7.5 PCE 或 BW/4HANA PCE 的迁移路径，简化过渡并更快地访问 BDC 服务，如数据产品、智能应用程序和 AI 功能。

9. BW 现代化的范围是什么，本地 BW 系统向 SAP BDC 过渡的推荐路径是什么？

BW 现代化是全面的，允许将整个 BW 环境逐步过渡到 BDC 和公共云组件。对于 HANA 上的本地 BW 7.5，推荐的路径是将其提升到由 SAP 管理的私有云版本 (PCE)，并受益于扩展维护和 BDC 的附加价值（数据产品、智能应用程序）。使用 BDC，您可以直接迁移并受益于扩展价值。将本地 BW 带到 PCE 的路径由 SAP 管理。您可以将本地 BW 集成到 SAP BDC 中，并逐步过渡。

10. BW 7.5 PCE 和 BW/4HANA PCE 的维护时间表是什么，BDC-BW-PCE 有终止日期吗？

SAP BW 7.5 PCE 合同可以运行到 2030 年底。BW/4HANA PCE 和本地版本与 S/4HANA 战略相关联（承诺至少到 2040 年）。SAP BW 容量服务遵循 SAP 的产品发布和维护战略。

11. SAP BW 的未来是什么，我还应该迁移到 BW/4HANA 吗？

使用 SAP BDC，建议是将本地 BW 系统提升到将由 SAP 管理的云端 (PCE)。您是否仍应迁移到 BW/4HANA 取决于您的需求，因为它将支持到 2040 年（基于 SAP BW/4HANA 版本）。使用 BDC，您可以按照自己的节奏迁移到 PCE。考虑 BW/4HANA 功能是否对您的用例至关重要。SAP BW bridge 将保留用于特殊用例。

12. SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 是否取代 SAP BW、Datasphere 和 Analytics Cloud？

不，SAP BDC 将 BW、Datasphere、SAC 和 SAP Databricks 的功能整合为一个完全托管的解决方案，因此它包含所有这些产品的功能，允许更容易的集成和过渡。

13. 与 SAP RISE PCE 相比，在 BDC 中使用 SAP BW、BW/4HANA PCE 有什么优势？

SAP BDC 提供了在云组件（如 BW、BW/4 PCE、Datasphere、SAC 和 Databricks）之间使用通用 BDC 容量单位重新分配资源的灵活性，无需重组合同。

14. SAP BDC 中的开发选项是什么，BW/ABAP 顾问需要新技能吗？

SAP BDC 建模使用与 SAP Datasphere 相同的工具（Data Builder、HDI Containers、OpenSQL schemas）。

15. SAP BW PCE 环境是否位于与一般 BDC 公共云相同的数据中心，还是可以位于其他地方？

SAP BW PCE 环境可以位于与其他 SAP BDC 组件分离的数据中心。但是，在大多数情况下，会优先考虑将 BW PCE 与 SAP S/4HANA PCE 系统共同定位。

16. SAP BW 或 BW/4HANA PCE 的大小限制是多少？

目前为 12TB，到 2025 年第二季度将增加到 24TB。

17. SAP BW 现代化计划提供什么？

提供免费的 SAP BW 云现代化评估。 提供转型激励；请咨询您的客户经理 (AE) 和客户成功经理 (CSM)。 也提供 SAP Factory 服务。

 

 

 

SAP Databricks:

1. 什么是 SAP Databricks，它如何增强 SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)？

SAP Databricks 是 Databricks 无服务器湖仓平台的完全 SAP 管理版本，从计算到存储，一切都完全集成并嵌入在 SAP BDC 内部。它利用 Apache Spark 进行大规模处理，无缝集成数据工程、机器学习和商业智能。这使您可以将结构化 SAP 数据与来自任何来源的非结构化和半结构化数据相结合。SAP Databricks 是一个定制的无服务器产品，其中 SAP 管理数据存储，Databricks 管理计算基础设施。这种合作为 BDC 客户提供了领先的 AI/ML 功能，补充了 Datasphere 和 SAC，并强化了 SAP 对开放数据生态系统的承诺。本质上，它弥合了传统业务数据和大数据之间的差距，赋能高级分析、AI/ML 和更深入的业务洞察。作为数据工程和 ML 平台的领导者，Databricks 显著增强了 BDC 的分析能力。

2. 与 Databricks 的合作如何运作？

SAP Databricks 作为核心组件直接嵌入 BDC 中——这是一个入站 OEM 产品。当您启用 BDC 时，您可以选择为 ML 和数据工程需求启用 Databricks。关键的是，这种集成允许在 BDC 和 Databricks 之间实现无缝、零复制、双向的数据产品共享。

3. 在 BDC 中，谁管理 SAP Databricks 使用的数据存储？

SAP 管理 Databricks 数据产品及其产生的任何结果的存储。这确保了数据安全、高效存储，并在 SAP 生态系统中维护 SAP 的数据治理。本质上，Databricks 提供计算引擎，但 SAP 控制和管理数据存储。

4. 在 SAP Business Data Cloud 中使用 SAP Databricks 的主要好处是什么？

SAP Databricks 提供几个关键好处：

通过 Delta Sharing 实现零复制数据共享：与 SAP Databricks 共享数据（无需复制），使用 Databricks 功能并与 BDC（SAP Datasphere）共享回来。 数据产品：访问 SAP 管理的数据产品，并组合和增强您的数据以创建有价值的新"派生"数据产品。然后，通过将它们发布到 BDC 数据产品目录和 Databricks Unity Catalog 来共享回来。 用于开发的专业代码工具：Databricks 的数据工程和 AI/ML 解决方案。 按需扩展：使用 Databricks SQL 快速轻松地分析海量数据。

5. 在向客户推介 SAP Databricks 时，绿地和棕地情况有什么区别？

绿地客户是指尚未使用 Databricks 或仅处理非 SAP 数据的客户。在这些情况下，SAP Databricks 被定位为处理 SAP 数据的解决方案。棕地客户是指已经使用原生/企业 Databricks 处理 SAP 数据的客户。对于这些客户，SAP 提供 SAP BDC 连接器，将其现有 Databricks 与 SAP BDC 集成，允许零复制数据集成。

6. 客户可以在 SAP Business Data Cloud 中使用其现有的企业/原生 Databricks 部署，或自带 Databricks 许可证吗？

是的，拥有现有 Databricks 安装的客户可以从 SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 中受益。为支持这一点，SAP 已于 2025 年 9 月发布了 SAP BDC 连接器。

  • 用于 Databricks 或外部系统的 SAP Business Data Cloud 连接器
  • 在 SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 和 Databricks 之间共享数据

7. SAP BDC 中 SAP Databricks 的定价结构是什么？

SAP Databricks 作为 SAP BDC 订阅的一部分包含在内，根据 BDC 容量单位计费。客户不需要购买单独的 Databricks 许可证；但是，他们需要确保有足够的容量单位来配置和使用其 BDC 订阅中的 SAP Databricks。

8. SAP Databricks 将如何影响企业的 AI/ML 能力？

SAP Databricks 通过为客户提供丰富的数据工程、机器学习和分析工具来增强 AI/ML 能力。客户可以开发安全和受治理的 AI/ML 解决方案，进行大规模数据分析，并利用 Databricks 的数据科学工作台和 Mosaic AI 来推动更深入的洞察和创新。

9. SAP Databricks 与标准/原生 Databricks 平台 (databricks.com) 不同吗？

是的，SAP Databricks 是通过 SAP 和 Databricks 之间的合作开发的专业版本。由于 BDC 数据产品是经过精选的，某些数据工程功能（如 Delta Live Tables 和 Workflows）不可用且不需要。

10. 我们如何确定 SAP Databricks 的正确大小？

Databricks 规模设定相当复杂，它包括各种容量服务，如无服务器计算、存储、模型服务、模型训练、出口等。一般的规模考虑将基于数据量、开发人员的 ETL/ML、ETL/SQL 工作负载和 AI/ML 工作负载。但是，一切都将在 SAP BDC 容量单位下进行简化，以方便我们的客户。计算器将很快提供。

11. 是否必须通过双向 delta-share 与 SAP BDC 共享回来？

这完全取决于是否在 databricks 中构建预测只是为了分析该数据集——可以使用 databricks SQL 功能，如数据探索，或者如果需要与 SAP 或自定义数据产品合并，那么您可以在 Datasphere 中构建模型并在 SAC 中创建故事。因此，这取决于个人喜好，如数据科学家或分析师，他们可以留在 databricks 中，或者他们可能会说我们只在 databricks 中完成 ML 部分并与 BDC 共享回来。

12. SAP Databricks 和原生/企业 Databricks 之间的 Delta share 功能相同吗？

不相同。SAP Databricks 只能与 SAP BDC 进行 delta-share，而 SAP Databricks 可以是来自 SAP BDC、Accuweather 和原生 Databricks 等的任何 delta-share 的接收者。而原生 databricks 对 delta-share 没有任何限制。

13. AWS Databricks 和 Azure Databricks 与 SAP Databricks 相同吗？

不，SAP Databricks 是 Databricks 的 SAP 管理版本，而 AWS Databricks、Azure Databricks 被视为原生/企业 Databricks。

 

 

 

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Connect:

1. 什么是 BDC Connect？

SAP BDC Connect 是一项新功能，可让您在 SAP 和合作伙伴系统之间安全共享数据，无需复制。这意味着您的数据和 AI 团队可以从双方实时访问 SAP 数据，无需构建或维护多个 ETL 管道。通过直接连接系统并按原样共享数据产品，SAP BDC Connect 消除了数据孤岛并创建了业务的统一视图。这使得使用一致、可信的数据运行分析、构建 AI/ML 模型和做出更好的决策变得更容易。

2. SAP BDC Connect 支持哪些合作伙伴系统？

• 企业 Databricks。 • Google BigQuery 目前处于公开预览阶段，计划于 2026 年上半年正式发布。 • Snowflake 目前处于公开预览阶段，计划于 2026 年上半年正式发布。 • Microsoft Fabric 目前处于公开预览阶段，计划于 2026 年下半年正式发布。

3. 客户可以在没有 SAP BDC 订阅的情况下使用 SAP BDC Connect 吗？

不可以，它仅适用于 SAP BDC 订阅。

4. 可以通过 SAP BDC Connect 共享什么数据？

SAP BDC Connect 支持数据产品的双向、零复制（delta-share）共享。SAP BDC 目录中显示的数据产品可以与合作伙伴系统共享，包括 SAP 管理的数据产品、客户管理的数据产品和合作伙伴构建的数据产品。

5. 使用 SAP BDC Connect 的费用是多少？

将收取名义费用作为 BDC CU 以涵盖数据传输费用（API 调用和跨区域）。

 

 

 

SAP Snowflake:

1. 什么是 SAP Snowflake？

解决方案扩展，提供完全托管的、全面的 snowflake 数据和 AI 功能。

结论：

我们已尽最大努力涵盖重要的常见问题解答，但我们理解您可能还有其他问题。请参阅标准 SAP 指南或随时使用评论部分提出问题。我们致力于使这个博客保持最新信息。希望这对您有所帮助。

entry.title: While creating QN attachment in API I am getting the authorization error

Kindly help me in resolving this issue

entry.title: Sequence in which QA10 transaction (RQEVAI30) picks the inspection lots for processing
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

We came across an issue where the QA10 program which ran via batch job daily picked two inspection lots and posted UD however, it picked the latest inspection lot first and then the older one.

The scenario is like, 1st a manual inspection lot was created for material/batch to record results of some MICs however, the business entered wrong values and decided to create a new inspection lot for the same material/batch combination. Now, there are two inspection lots for material/batch but with two different values in MIC. Once the batch job for QA10 ran, it updated the batch characteristics from the 1st manual inspection lot. This created a panic within users, and they reached out to us.

Upon checking the logs in QA10L, it showed that - the QA10 program picked the 2nd lot first for posting UD and then the 1st lot. So, the value of MIC from 1st lot was updated in batch characteristics.

So, wanted to know how does the program picks lots for processing auto UD and is there a way to make it pick in sequence of lot creation.

Thanks a lot, in advance.

Best Regards,

Omm

entry.title: Re: Are the PM Fiori apps also applicable for CS?
entry.content:
Greetings,

unfortunately some Fiori applications are only available from phase based processes. You can always run transactions in browser (GUI for HTML).

I think the customer service (CS) as it was known is in the past is being replaced by Service processes. Take a look at the following best practice scenarios in process navaigator:

  • 41Z - Service Quotation and Order Management
  • 6AU - Corrective Service with Advanced Execution
  • 7GB - Service - In-House Repair
  • 6F1 - Preventive Service with Advanced Execution
  • 426 - Service Contract Management

 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/c9b5e9de6e674fb99fff88d72c352291/30284625681843518ba7cfdedaebc530.html?locale=en-US

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: Can we round off the merit amount using standard meri...
entry.content: What you need to do is to use the multiple function for the number formats.   For example, if you want to round all the merits to the nearest dollar, use a multiple of 1 and remove the decimals from the formats.   This will round all the money fields to the nearest dollar, however, that is not usually an issue with that type of requirement

entry.title: When Innovation Meets Real Business Impact - VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026
entry.content:
A community powered by creativity

The VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon has once again shown what happens when passionate people, real business challenges, and SAP Business Technology Platform come together. Teams of customers, partners, and experts turned ideas into tangible solutions that demonstrate how SAP BTP can drive measurable value in day-to-day operations.​

Over the course of the Hackathon, every team brought its own perspective, business context and technical skills – from integration and automation to SAP Business AI and extensions on SAP BTP.

The result: a set of working prototypes that not only impress technically, but are rooted in real customer needs and adoption potential.

Celebrating the 2026 finalists

After intense jury deliberation, three teams earned a spot in the grand final at SYNC 2026 for their strong presentations, clear business impact, and high potential for real-world rollout.​

  • Promocean (supported by SOA People) – “Operational Excellence through SAP Business AI”: A showcase of how SAP BTP and Business AI can streamline operations and enhance decision-making in a very pragmatic way.​

  • Ecotone (supported by Expertum) – “Interface Maintenance”: A smart solution addressing the often underestimated pain of interface management, using SAP BTP to bring transparency, stability and control.​

  • NTT Data – “ProTrack”: A forward-looking use case that leverages SAP BTP to better track, steer, or optimize processes​

These finalists did not just deliver “cool demos”, they delivered stories where business and technology reinforce each other, with SAP BTP at the core as the innovation platform.

A strong field beyond the finalists

Of course, a hackathon is about much more than just three winners. Aarini Consulting, Nederlandse Spoorwegen, and Simac IT & BSC also delivered impressive solutions that made the jury’s job anything but easy.​

Each of these teams tackled real business scenarios and used SAP BTP services to create innovation that can move quickly from prototype to production. The organizers explicitly encourage all teams to work with their coaches on post-hackathon follow-up to drive implementation and adoption, because the real success of a hackathon is measured when the ideas go live.

SYNC 2026: where the stage is yours

As recognition of this milestone, the three finalist teams are invited to present at SYNC 2026, the VNSG Annual Conference on March 12. During the opening session, each finalist will pitch their solution live on stage to the SYNC audience. The audience will then vote to determine the VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon Champion – a great opportunity to showcase innovation, customer impact and the power of SAP BTP in front of the wider SAP community.

Keep the momentum going

For everyone involved – finalists, other teams, coaches and the broader community – this hackathon is a starting point, not an ending. The next steps are about refining solutions, planning production rollouts, and scaling adoption so that the innovation created during the hackathon translates into lasting business impact.​

If you are part of the VNSG or wider SAP community and feel inspired by these stories, take this as a call to action: bring your own business challenges, experiment on SAP BTP and co-innovate with partners and customers.

The SYNC 2026 VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon has proven once more that with the right mix of creativity, collaboration and platform capabilities, you can turn ideas into value – fast...

SAP Business Technology Platform SAP Build SAP Integration Suite

@hansvp @qmrjvd @sricsi98 @BvE @mdschoenmakers @tamasszirtes @f_van_leeuwen @Petra1 @BartvdKamp @tedcastelijns @Arjan_deMol @dvvelzen @JdTeuling @AnnikaHeus @LaurensSteffers

entry.title: SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 3: AI (Beta) Innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602
entry.content:
This third post in my series gives an overview of the very latest AI-related user experience innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602, which are so new that they are only released for customers to try them out via beta testing. Particular highlights are the planned AI-assisted Task Automation via “smart helpers” and the AI-assisted Easy Fill for SAP Fiori elements-based applications. Plus many exciting application-specific UX innovations, where AI helps users get their work done more easily.

In case you missed them, Part 2 of my series covers the generally available UX innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602, and Part 1 provides general updates about Joule, SAP Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start.

I’ve grouped the AI innovations currently in beta testing into four main sections.

  • Cross-area UX innovations.
  • Application-specific Joule agents.
  • Application-specific AI for helping users deal with errors.
  • Application-specific embedded AI use cases.

The third section features some Joule agents as well as some embedded AI use cases, all related to helping users deal with errors.

 

Cross-Area UX Innovations

AI-assisted task automation is an exciting brand new innovation for the 2602 release, planned to become available for beta testing in May with 2602.3, but you can sign up for the beta program already today. The other two innovations, AI-assisted smart solution for situations in My Home and AI-assisted easy fill of SAP Fiori elements-based applications are already available for you to try out. You can try out any of them by signing up here.

Beta: AI-Assisted Task Automation

The planned AI-assisted task automation (beta) allows your key users and end users to automate repetitive activities based on SAP Fiori apps with so-called ”smart helpers”. They are created in a design time and accessed through My Home. What makes this approach so powerful is that you simply need to describe your desired business outcome in natural language, related to a specific SAP Fiori elements-based application, and the smart helper will take care of it for you.

Let us take the example shown in Figure 1: for the application Create Sales Orders – Automatic Extraction, the user has invoked the task automation application Manage Smart Helpers and entered “Select sales order requests with status complete. Simulate the sales order and where the simulation is successful, create the sales order”.

Based on this instruction, SAP Business AI creates an execution plan and shows it to the user, as you can see in Figure 1 too, where the application is listed as well as the proposed actions to execute.

Figure 1: Beta: Manage Smart Helpers application for defining instructions for the planned AI-assisted task automation. Alt Text: Screenshot showing the instruction text at the top, an empty section for attachments, and the execution plan with four steps: read order requests, analyze request data, simulate pending requests, create sales orders.

Figure 2 shows how we currently envisage that users will get an overview of the smart helpers that they have created.

Figure 2: Beta: The planned way of giving users an overview of the AI-assisted task automation smart helpers in My Home. Alt Text: Screenshot of My Home with a section “Smart Helpers” above the “Apps” section, with two tabs “Smart Helpers (4)” and “Active Runs (3)”. The latter is selected, showing three cards with title, description and executed step shown: one is completed, two are running.

Have a look at this video to get a better idea of how this will work:

  • Video: AI-Assisted Task Automation (2:00 minutes).

Beta: AI-Assisted Smart Solution for Situations in My Home

With this feature, you can now get details of a Business Situation shown directly in place on My Home, and even initiate quick actions or make changes to business data there. This is achieved by expanding the Situation card on My Home when you click on it, as you can see in Figure 3, or in action in this video:

  • Video: AI-Assisted Smart Solution for Situations in My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (0:50 min.)

Figure 3: Beta: AI-Assisted Smart Solution for Situations in My Home. Alt Text: An image with two screenshots: the upper one show the top part of My Home showing five Situation cards in the To-Dos section. One titled “End Date for Project Digital Media Assessment for Media Corp…” is linked with an arrow to an expanded card shown in the second screenshot – the card is taller and wider, contains a summary on the left with two action buttons “Assign To Me” and “Close Situation”, and more details on the right.

Beta: AI-Assisted Easy Fill

Users often receive requests via e-mails or chats to change data in the system. This new feature makes it much easier to update the fields in the various sections of an object page based on SAP Fiori elements, such as a sales order. Instead of manually scanning the e-mail and navigating to the relevant sections and fields in the object page, you can just paste the text into Easy Fill and it will automatically identify the correct fields to update, with proposals for the correct entries.

The power of AI means that the new values do not have to be exactly contained in the text. Figure 4 shows an example, where the new address is given without actually mentioning the name of the city, only the street and post code, along with an abbreviation for the state and country (“BW DE”). As you can see, the proposed new values are correct, despite this missing or abbreviated information.

Figure 4: Beta: AI-Assisted Easy Fill for SAP Fiori elements based applications. Alt-Text: A screenshot showing an Easy Fill pop-over, with a text entry field on the left with text from a customer e-mail informing about a change of name and address for their store, and on the left showing the “Filled Fields”, a list of seven fields for which new values are proposed.

Watch a demo:

  • Video: AI-Assisted Easy Fill of SAP Fiori Elements-Based Applications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (1:15 min.)

Note that this feature is only available for applications based on SAP Fiori elements.

 

Application-Specific Joule Agents

Agents that are specialized on specific application use cases can be particularly effective at helping users get their work done more easily. As you can see with these examples, good use of AI has considerable benefits for user experience.

The first two agents that I have highlighted below are brand new in the beta program with the 2602 release.

Beta: Project Setup Agent

This is new with the 2602 release, available now for beta testing by signing up here. This agent helps project managers create a new project based on a reference project. It also allows them to staff key resources directly and release the project for time booking. By guiding the user step by step, Joule helps to streamline the process and ensure that it is free of errors, so that project managers can spend more time on their projects rather than on administration.

Figure 5: Beta: Project Setup Agent. Alt Text: Four screenshots of Joule. 1) the user prompts “Create Project”; Joule asks for detail like the name. 2) Joule found a similar project. 3) Project successfully created, user prompts “Staff Users”, Joule asks who to staff. 4) User lists names, Joule says Nancy is already booked, user says keep the position open, and ask Joule to notify the staff.

Figure 5 above shows an example, you can dig deeper by watching this video:

  • Project Setup Agent in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (1:33 minutes).

Beta: Dispute Resolution Agent

This is also new with the 2602 release, available now for beta testing by signing up here.

In an ideal world, customers pay all your invoices on time, no questions asked. In the real world, customers sometimes dispute payments, claiming that the invoices are incorrect. The new dispute resolution agent helps contract accountants identify and resolve disputes by analyzing both the invoice details and the contractual terms and identifying discrepancies, errors, or mismatches. Based on this analysis, it then suggests how to proceed to resolve the dispute.

Figure 6 shows an example, where the customer has complained that the invoiced amount is too high. Analysis by the dispute resolution agent reveals that the invoice is indeed too high, more than the 2% annual rise in service basis fee stipulated in the contract. It then recommends creating a credit memo.

Figure 6: Beta: Dispute Resolution Agent. Alt Text: Screenshot showing a dispute case full screen application, with Joule opened. User prompted “Help me with dispute 40000000171”, Joule replied with sections Overview, Discrepency Identified, Finding, I recommend, each containing detailed text.

Watch it in action and find out more:

  • Video: Joule Agents: Dispute Resolution Agent (1:38 minutes).
  • SAP Discovery Center: Dispute Resolution Agent

Beta: Accounts Receivable Agent (soon GA)

This agent helps accounts receivable (AR) accountants to reduce Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) by prioritizing the closure of open items and highlighting high-value follow-up actions. It analyses receivables data across customer balances, dunning history, and dispute items and makes recommendations based on these data-backed insights.

Overall, the agent reduces the amount of time AR accountants need to spend reconciling and managing receivables. Figure 7 shows an example of an analysis of a specified customer account along with the recommended actions.

The beta-testing program is closed for this agent, we expect it to become generally available in the next month or two.

Figure 7: Beta: Accounts Receivable Agent. Alt Text: Two images of Joule. On the left the user has asked “Analyze overdue balances for GreenWheel Inc….”, Joule shows a card “Analysis” with a list including totals, disputed amount, non-disputed overdue. On the right the Dispute Case Status is shown, and the user asked “Show Recommended Actions”.

Watch the video and read more:

  • Video: Accounts Receivable Agent (3:19 minutes)
  • SAP Discovery Center: Accounts Receivable Agent.

Beta: Accounting Accruals Agent

An accrual is an accounting entry which represents revenue which has been earned or expenses which have incurred but for which no payment has yet been made. That way, when looking at a particular period such as a quarter or a month, accountants get a more accurate picture of a company’s financial position. For example, if a service was delivered, but not paid for within the period, having the accrual means that the cost of delivering the service and the revenue associated with it both appear in the summary for the period.

Calculating accruals and deferrals is often time very time consuming. It can also require estimates based on historical data, if for example invoices have not yet been created, or payment amounts can be reduced if payments are made quickly in the future.

The new accounting accruals agent can significantly speed this up, potentially reducing the time spent on these activities by 80%! This frees up accountants to work on more value-adding activities, such as analysis. As you can see in Figure 8, the agent creates a list of accrual proposals. For each proposal, you can see details on the recommendation, including an explanation for the basis of the recommendation.

Figure 8: Beta: Accrual Proposals made by the accounting accruals agent. Alt Text: A screenshot of the Accrual Proposals applications, showing a list of six accrual proposals on the left. The details of the one selected are shown on the right, showing the recommended accrual amount, the proposed line items and a section “Basis of AI Recommendation” with a natural language explanation along with a bar chart and a forecast line for the trend per month.

Watch this video to find out more:

  • Video: Accounting Accruals Agent (1:36 min.)
  • SAP Discovery Center: Accounting Accruals Agent.

Beta: Cash Management Agent

Cash managers need to manage the company’s liquidity, by transferring money to accounts that are in the red and making informed decisions on investment or borrowing opportunities. With the Cash Management Agent, Joule gives the cash manager an overview of the current cash position by collecting opening balances and expected cash flows, and then calculating expected closing balances. Joule automatically identifies potential cash shortages and surpluses according the customer’s treasury policy, and proposes appropriate bank transfers. This reduces the time needed to retrieve the relevant data and make informed decisions significantly.

Figure 9 shows an example where the cash manager asks “Show me my priorities today”, Joule provides the daily cash position summary with the main negative and positive accounts, and makes proposals for bank transfers.

Figure 9: Beta: Cash Management Agent. Alt Text: Six images of Joule, on the very left the start screen with options as buttons. 1) The user chooses “Show me my priorities today”. 2) shows a Daily Cash Position Summary card, with tables for positive and negative balance accounts; 3) shows the user has scrolled horizontally in the table to show the opening balances. 4) Joule shows bank transfer proposals, each with a “Select” button. 5) Details of the one the user chose. 6) User entered “Confirm and Create”, Joule informs successfully created.

Find out more here:

  • Video: Cash Management Agent
  • SAP Discovery Center: Cash Management Agent

Beta: Production Planning and Operations Agent

This agent helps production planners save time releasing production orders. It checks material, capacity and scheduling availability. If there are no issues, the orders can be released immediately. Where it helps most is in cases where there are material shortages: it suggests workarounds such as alternative components or materials or adjusting schedules. Once all the criteria are satisfied, the planner can release the order directly within Joule, and the agent will complete the production order release.

Figure 10: Beta: Production Planning and Operations Agent. Alt Text: Screenshot of the “Manage Product Orders” app, with Joule opened, stating that a production order has been released, but a component is not available, and suggests to reschedule to a later date. The user entered “Reschedule and release”, and Joule confirmed success.

See it in action and read about it:

  • Video: Production Planning and Operations Agent
  • SAP Discovery Center: Production Planning and Operations Agent.

If you are interested in trying this out, you can sign up here.

Beta: International Trade Classifications Agent

I am highlighting this because it can save users 50% of their time, in some cases up to 80%! (as verified by user research with our first customers). The job to be done here by product classification specialists is to classify products that are to be shipped abroad. They need to assign the correct customs tariff numbers and commodity codes according to trade regulations, and provide a rationale for their decision so that the whole process is compliant and will hold up in an audit.

The process is labor intensive and error prone, since a few trade compliance specialists typically need to handle a high number of complex products (goods, software, services).

The International Trade Classifications Agent helps enormously here, since it looks at a product’s characteristics as maintained in the system, and then recommends the appropriate classification, with an easy to understand rationale for the recommendation. Even better: if there are potentially many classifications, it will rank them and provide a rationale for each one. Figure 11 shows an example for a fitness watch that needs to be classified.

Figure 11: Beta: International Trade Classifications Agent. Alt Text: A screenshot of Classify Products, with a list of four AI-generated proposals for product classification, each with a “Select Proposal” button.

Watch this video to get the full picture, using the fitness watch as an example:

  • Video: International Trade Classification Agent (3:50 minutes).
  • SAP Discovery Center: International Trade Classification Agent.

If you are interested in trying this out, you can sign up here.

Beta: Maintenance Planner Agent (Soon GA)

This agent helps maintenance planners manage and prioritize maintenance tasks, thus ensuring equipment reliability and minimizing downtime.

  • Video: SAP S/4HANA Asset Management – Maintenance Planner Agent (2:41 minutes).
  • SAP Discovery Center: Maintenance Planner Agent.

The beta-testing program is closed for this agent, we expect it to become generally available in the next month or two.

 

Application-Specific AI for Helping Users Deal with Errors

Dealing with errors takes a lot of time and effort, and can also be frustrating. Hence we have identified areas where AI can help. In my previous blog post I featured the AI-Assisted Error Explanation which is generally available now. This feature uses AI to explain error messages from the system when incorrect data is entered into the application.

Application-specific errors are more complex to deal with, since they turn up when a process has started and subsequently runs into issues. This example of AI-assisted error resolution using Joule is already generally available:

  • AI-assisted posting issue handling for billing documents
    supports tasks related to resolving such issues and managing billing documents, including providing resolutions for posting errors caused by an unopened posting period, enabling the posting of valid billing documents, and allowing users to modify the billing date of existing billing documents as needed.
    • Video: Process Billing Documents with Posting Issues (2:47 minutes).
    • Blog post: How to Handle Posting Issues for Billing Documents Using Joule.

AI-Assisted Error Resolution for Product Cost Accounting

This is new for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602, planned to be available with the Joule 2603 release in March.

Errors are brought to a user’s attention via Intelligent Situation Handling notifications and To-Dos on My Home, but users can also directly ask Joule to “show my errors in company code XXX” to get an overview. Joule will display the errors , help you discover the root cause and guide you through the resolution with clear, step‑by‑step instructions, as shown in Figure 12.

Figure 12: AI-Assisted Error Resolution for Cost Accounting (planned availability with Joule 2603 release). Alt Text: Five Joule images, starting with the list of situations, then showing the Explanation and Resolution content, which spans the other four images, with three detailed steps for the user, and at the end links to apps to use. Find out more here:

  • Video: AI-Assisted Error Resolution for Cost Accounting (2:00 minutes).
  • Documentation: AI-Assisted Error Resolution.

Beta: AI-Assisted Payment Exception Analysis

Most payments to vendors are automated, the first step being the payment proposal run which checks for payments that are ready to be made. Errors that surface after such a run need to be checked and resolved manually, which can be time consuming. This is where the new payment exception analysis comes in handy: it helps accounts payable (AP) accountants to identify the root cause of the error, giving them the context,  and a step-by-step guide for resolving the issue. Figure 13 below shows an example.

Figure 13: Beta: AI-Assisted Payment Exception Analysis. Alt Text: Screenshot of the Revise Payment Proposals app with an Exception Analysis and Resolution pop-over, with sections “Analysis”, “Resolution” and “Logs”.

 Watch it in action and find out more:

  • Video: AI-Assisted Payment Exception Analysis (1:21 minutes)
  • SAP Discovery Center: Payment Exception Analysis.

 

Application-Specific Embedded AI Use Cases

Beyond dealing with errors and exceptions, we also have a number of interesting cases available for beta testing where AI helps users get their work done directly within their application. I have chosen to feature just one of these, but after that you will find links to more examples.

Beta: EHS Permit Management

This new feature also has huge potential for saving effort and cost: we estimate 65% cost reduction in environmental permit management cost, and 80% reduction in environmental management penalties and fines. In other words: a huge improvement in user experience and user satisfaction!

Environmental, health and safety regulations can range from a few pages to hundreds of pages, depending on what it is regulating. Checking these documents and ensuring that your company is compliant, with all the necessary tasks in place to stay compliant, is a tedious and time-consuming task.

This new permit management feature automatically extracts and sets up an EHS- permit from PDF documents. It identifies all the requirements and makes proposals for the necessary tasks, ranging from simple ones to maintenance notification tasks and reporting tasks. The user can flip through the regulatory document in the left side of the screen. For each page that they look at, they see highlighted the part of the document containing a concrete requirement, and on the right side of the screen a list of proposed tasks. With one click, the user can select a proposed task and create an actual task. Figure 14 shows an example.

Figure 14: AI-Assisted Permit Management for SAP EHS Management, environment  management. Alt Text: Screenshot showing one page of a PDF document on the left with a paragraph higlighted, and a list of proposed tasks on the right.

Have a look at this video to see it in action, in particular how users can select from the proposed tasks to create tasks:

  • Video: EHS Permit Management.
  • SAP Discovery Center: EHS Permit Management.

Additional Use Cases for Embedded AI in Beta

Here are three more examples for AI embedded in applications, helping users get their work done more easily.

Beta: AI-Assisted Compliance Information Processing

With this innovation, master data specialists can automatically process supplier compliance disclosures information such as regulations, status and upstream suppliers data, and map it to compliance requirements in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for product compliance.

  • Video (1:28 minutes) and SAP Discovery Center entry.

Beta: AI-Assisted Safety Instruction Generation

This innovation for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, EHS workplace safety, automatically generates clear, structured and easy-to-understand safety instructions for specific equipment.

  • Video (1:35 minutes) and SAP Discovery Center entry.

 

Continued soon …

I hope you enjoyed this third part, covering the new UX innovations available for beta testing with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508. I will post part four in the next few days, covering exciting new UX innovations available in SAP Success Factors.

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

entry.title: Re: Accout code which is posted by FAGL_FCV automatically
entry.content:
Hi @KentaroTakahashi ,
Yes, it is possible to control or change the account codes that FAGL_FCV (Foreign Currency Valuation in New GL) uses for posting valuation gains and losses. The account determination is not hardcoded—it is driven by customizing settings in SAP.

Transaction FAGL_FCV posts valuation differences automatically. The account determination depends on customizing in valuation areas and account assignment settings. You can define which G/L accounts are used for:
1. Exchange rate gains/losses (realized/unrealized)
2. Balance sheet adjustment accounts
3. Valuation differences

Please follow the configuration steps below.
SPRO → Financial Accounting → General Ledger Accounting → Periodic Processing → Valuation → Define Valuation Areas, Each valuation area can have its own rules. You assign valuation methods and specify how postings should be made.

SPRO → Financial Accounting → General Ledger Accounting → Periodic Processing → Valuation → Define Accounts for Realized/Unrealized Exchange Rate Differences
Here you assign the specific G/L accounts for gains and losses.
You can differentiate by account type (e.g., balance sheet vs. P&L).

Valuation Method Settings, Within the valuation method, you define how the system determines accounts.
You can assign different accounts per valuation area.

Change the G/L accounts for valuation postings by updating the customizing in the valuation method/account determination. Assign different accounts per valuation area if you want separate postings for different reporting requirements. Ensure that the valuation area you are using in FAGL_FCV is linked to the correct account determination.

Hope you got what you are looking for. If yes, then accept my solution

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Network on supporting regulatory processing of e-invoices in France
entry.content:
Regulatory Change Manager Tool

Regulatory Change Manager Tool helps with providing timely information on upcoming regulatory changes that can affect SAP products. You can access the tool here.

It is a SaaS application built on SAP Business Technology Platform that provides a centralized repository of global regulatory changes relevant to business processes and SAP products. It serves as a one-stop solution for all regulatory change requirements, offering comprehensive and well-organized information to help organizations rapidly adapt to new and evolving regulations.

Use Cases in Invoicing:

  • Tax Regulation Changes: Monitors changes in tax requirements that affect invoice processing and calculation methods
  • Invoice Format Requirements: Tracks mandatory e-invoicing format changes and submission requirements across different countries
  • Compliance Reporting: Supports preparation of statutory reports and ensures alignment with evolving regulatory frameworks

For more details, refer What Is the Regulatory Change Manager Tool?

entry.title: Developing HANA ML models with SAP Databricks
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP HANA provides a rich set of Machine Learning capabilities natively which can be used via SQL or python interface. For an introduction to these capabilities you can refer to HANA Machine Learning and Developing Regression Models with the Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA learning journey and this excellent blog post from @YannickSchaper.

In this blogpost I will walkthrough the capabilities that enhance the power of hana-ml with the model tracking capabilities provided by  mlflow . The python package hana-ml has supported the tracking and usability of trained ml models via mlflow which are covered extensively in these 2-part blogposts tracking-hana-machine-learning-experiments-with-mlflow-a-conceptual-guide from @stojanm and @martinboeckling . In this post I will focus on the Databricks managed mlflow as it greatly eases the use of mlflow without having to setup the mlflow server. These capabilities are available both in SAP Databricks from SAP Business Data Cloud(BDC) and Enterprise Databricks for customers who connect Databricks to BDC via bdc-connect. For this blogpost I will be using SAP Databricks provisioned with SAP Business Data Cloud.

With the launch of SAP Business Data Cloud, developers have a much more streamlined access to AI/ML capabilities both from SAP and Databricks. This applies to data available via the Unity Catalog or accessible via the SQL access. Here I will focus on the Notebook capabilities and Training and Inference on datasets in the HANA Cloud layer accessed via SQL and utilize the compute of HANA Cloud.

 

The datasets in HANA Cloud could be data persisted in HANA or remotely available via federation from HDLFS or BDC Data Products installed to embedded HANA Cloud from Datasphere.

Connect to ML datasets on HANA Cloud

To connect to data on the HANA Cloud, be it the embedded HANA Cloud of SAP Datasphere or a stand-alone HANA Cloud, one needs the 4 parameters which provide the url, port(443), username and password.

Prerequisites

In addition the HANA Cloud or Datasphere instance needs to have the Databricks IP to the Allow-list to enable connection.

The database user needs to have the following privileges which are provided by the HANA Cloud or Datasphere administrator 

  1. AFL__SYS_AFL_AFLPAL_EXECUTE_WITH_GRANT_OPTION
  2. AFL__SYS_AFL_APL_AREA_EXECUTE
  3. AFLPM_CREATOR_ERASER_EXECUTE

For Datasphere these privileges are enabled when the administrator creates the database user with OpenSQL access and Enables APL and PAL

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Connect to HANA from Databricks

Here is a code snippet to connect to the HANA Cloud SQL layer for data access using Databricks secret. For this you need to create the secrets needed for HANA Cloud connectivity like snippet below

from databricks.sdk import WorkspaceClient
w = WorkspaceClient() 
scope = "<scope-name>"
w.secrets.create_scope(scope)
url =  "<hana-url>"
port = 443
user = "<hana-db-user>"
password = "<hana-db-password>"
w.secrets.put_secret(scope,"hana_url",string_value =url)
w.secrets.put_secret(scope,"hana_port",string_value =port)
w.secrets.put_secret(scope,"hana_user",string_value =user)
w.secrets.put_secret(scope,"hana_password",string_value = password)

create_secrets

import os
import hana_ml
from hana_ml import dataframe
import mlflow

print("hana_ml version:", hana_ml.__version__)
print("mlflow version:", mlflow.__version__)
scope = "<scope_name>"
os.environ['HANA_ADDRESS'] = dbutils.secrets.get(scope=scope, key="hana_url")
os.environ['HANA_PORT']    = dbutils.secrets.get(scope=scope, key="hana_port")
os.environ['HANA_UNAME']   = dbutils.secrets.get(scope=scope, key="hana_user")
os.environ['HANA_PASS']    = dbutils.secrets.get(scope=scope, key="hana_password")
import hana_ml.dataframe as dataframe
cc = dataframe.ConnectionContext(
    address=os.environ['HANA_ADDRESS'],
    port=os.environ['HANA_PORT'],
    user=os.environ['HANA_UNAME'],
    password=os.environ['HANA_PASS']
)
if cc.connection.isconnected(): 
    print(f'User {os.environ["HANA_UNAME"]} connected to HANA successfully')
    print(f"HANA Version: {cc.hana_version()}")

Otherwise you can also connect via the python-dotenv, especially if you are developing locally.

 

Develop the ML model with mlflow

Here I will use a sample dataset provided by hana-ml package to make it easier to test. This would be replaced by the appropriate dataset the user wants to use for training the ML model.

from hana_ml.algorithms.pal.utility import DataSets
# Load Dataset
bike_dataset = DataSets.load_bike_data(cc)#This creates the correspoding table on HANA Cloud 
# number of rows and number of columns
print("Shape of datset: {}".format(bike_dataset.shape))
# columns
print(bike_dataset.columns)
# types of each column
print(bike_dataset.dtypes())

# print the first 3 rows of dataset
print(bike_dataset.head(3).collect())

#Split the dataset into train & test
# Add a ID column for AutomaticRegression, the last column is the label
bike_dataset = bike_dataset.add_id('ID', ref_col='days_since_2011')

# Split the dataset into training and test dataset
cols = bike_dataset.columns
cols.remove('cnt')
bike_data = bike_dataset[cols + ['cnt']]
bike_train = bike_data.filter('ID <= 600')
bike_test = bike_data.filter('ID > 600')
print(bike_train.head(3).collect())
print(bike_test.head(3).collect())

We used a basic splitting methodology above, hana-ml provides splitting capabilities via hana_ml.algorithms.pal.partition.train_test_val_split to assist in this process.

Now that we have  a training and test dataset, we can start the training process and use mlflow to track the results in Databricks experiments via the code below

mlflow.set_tracking_uri("databricks")
experiment_path = '<experiment_path>' 
mlflow.set_experiment(experiment_path)
# Here we are using AutomaticRegression to show the metrics automatically created and tracked via mlflow
from hana_ml.algorithms.pal.auto_ml import AutomaticClassification, AutomaticRegression
auto_r = AutomaticRegression(generations=2,
                             population_size=15,
                             offspring_size=5)

# enable_workload_class if you have workload_classes defined on HANA Cloud instance, here we disable it but in productive scenarios you would have it enabled
#auto_r.enable_workload_class(workload_class_name="PAL_AUTOML_WORKLOAD")
auto_r.disable_workload_class_check()
try:
    with mlflow.start_run(run_name="hana-ml-autoreg-bike") as run:
        auto_r.enable_mlflow_autologging(is_exported=True)
        auto_r.fit(bike_train, key="ID")
        runid = run.info.run_id
except Exception as e:
    raise e

The enable_mlflow_autologging function above enables the creation of key model metrics automatically, in this case suitable for regression without any additional effort from the user. These metrics would differ based on the algorithm. The user can easily log additional parameters, metrics, artifacts as desired and supported my mlfow.

When the above code is run we get the experiments logged with default metrics that hana-ml model logged automatically via mlflow for example R2, RMSE etc below.

Databricks Experiment and mlflow with hana-ml

One can then compare different runs and track model progression as parameters are changed.Compare hana-ml mlflow runs

 

For inferencing on data the hana-ml model can be loaded to HANA Cloud via the code below, the run_id is the run from the Databricks Experiments that you would like to use for inference and can be obtained from the overview of the Experiment

from hana_ml.model_storage import ModelStorage
bikemodel = ModelStorage.load_mlflow_model(connection_context=cc, model_uri='runs:/{}/model'.format(runid))

#Get the info for the loaded model
bikemodel.mlflow_model_info

#Use the trained model for prediction on test or new dataset
res = bikemodel.predict(bike_test.deselect('cnt') , key="ID")
print(res.collect())
bike_test.deselect('cnt').save("INFERENCE_BIKE_DATA_TBL") #Saving this here for using later via the Serving Endpoint

 

 

Serve the ML model for inferencing

The hana-ml model can be served on BTP if desirable by exporting the mlmodel. Alternatively, it can be served on Databricks via the Serving endpoint.

Databricks does not natively support the serving for the hana-ml model. However, this can be achieved via the mlflow.pyfunc functionality to provide custom models. I will be using the "model from code" method as it has advantages over the legacy methods and recommended going forward. This requires passing the custom handler as separate code. For this we write the python file which handles the desired input to the serving endpoint. In my example, I want the user to pass in the name of table which exists in HANA Cloud(in our example we saved it as  

INFERENCE_BIKE_DATA_TBL

and has the data that needs to be inferenced. The user sends the name of the table to the inference endpoint. The code can be modified to have the input say as a payload to the inference end-point, in that case the custom handler function(hana_ml_pyfunc_model) would then need to persist the payload as a HANA table so the hana-ml predict can be called on it.

Create the custom handler for hana-ml

# Save as script: hana_ml_pyfunc_model.py
# %%writefile "./hana_ml_pyfunc_model.py"
import mlflow
from mlflow import pyfunc
from mlflow.models import set_model
import hana_ml
from hana_ml import dataframe
from hana_ml.model_storage import ModelStorage
import os


class hana_ml_pyfunc_model(pyfunc.PythonModel):

    def connectToHANA(self, context):
        try:
            url =  os.getenv('hana_url') 
            port = os.getenv('hana_port')
            user = os.getenv('hana_user')
            passwd = os.getenv('hana_password')
            connection_context = dataframe.ConnectionContext(url, port, user, passwd)
            return connection_context
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Exception occurred: {e}")
            raise e
            return "Exception:{e}", e
    @mlflow.trace
    def load_context(self, context):
        try: 
            with mlflow.start_span("load_context"):
                self.model = context.artifacts["model"]
                self.connection_context = self.connectToHANA(context)
                print("HANA_ML_MODEL loaded in load_context")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Exception occurred: {e}")
            raise Exception(f"Loading the context failed due to {e}")
    @mlflow.trace
    def predict(self, context, model_input):
        table_name = None
        try: 
            if self.connection_context.connection.isconnected() == False:
                with mlflow.start_span("connect_to_HANA"):
                    self.connection_context = self.connectToHANA(context)
                    if self.connection_context.connection.isconnected():
                        print("HANA Connection Successful")
                    else:
                        raise Exception("HANA Connection Failed")
       
            with mlflow.start_span("load_model"):
                hana_model = ModelStorage.load_mlflow_model(connection_context=self.connection_context, model_uri=self.model,use_temporary_table=False, force=True)
           
                print("HANA_ML_MODEL loaded in predict")
                print("model_input", model_input)
                table_name = str(model_input["INFERENCE_TABLE_NAME"][0]) 
                print("Table Name:", table_name)
            with mlflow.start_span("hana_ml_predict"):
                df = self.connection_context.table(table_name)
                if df.count() > 0:
                  
                    print(f"Running HANA ML inference on {table_name} with {df.count()} records")
                    prediction = hana_model.predict(df, key = "ID").collect()
                    print("Prediction completed")
                else:
                    raise Exception(f"HANA Inference Table {table_name} is empty")
        
            return prediction
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Exception occurred: {e}")
            raise f"Exception:{e}"
        
set_model(hana_ml_pyfunc_model())

 

Log the custom pyfunc model

Then we log the above pyfunc model which can be registered to enable the creation of a Serving endpoint on Databricks

#Create the signature for the model input and output. In this example:
# the input is the name of an existing table in HANA which has the data that needs to be inferenced 
# the output is the "cnt" counts for the bike data and the associated score
import mlflow
from mlflow.models import ModelSignature, infer_signature
from mlflow.types.schema import Schema, ColSpec
signature = ModelSignature(inputs = Schema([ColSpec("string", "INFERENCE_TABLE_NAME")]))
signature.outputs = Schema([ColSpec("integer", "ID"), ColSpec("double", "SCORES")])


mlflow.set_tracking_uri("databricks")
runid="<run_id>" #runid from the training phase which is the chosen champion model to be served
model_uri='runs:/{}/model'.format(runid)
experiment_name = '<experiment_name>' 
mlflow.set_experiment(experiment_name)

model_file = "hana_ml_pyfunc_model.py" #This is the file that is written in step above and handles the calll to hana_ml for predict on the user-provided inference table

with mlflow.start_run() as run:
    mlflow.pyfunc.log_model(
            artifact_path="model",
            python_model=model_file,
            artifacts={"model": model_uri},
            pip_requirements=["hana-ml","ipython"],
            signature = signature,
            input_example={"INFERENCE_TABLE_NAME" : "INFERENCE_BIKE_DATA_TBL"},
    )
# Register the model #Need to register the model to enable it to be served on Databricks
model_uri = f"runs:/{run.info.run_id}/model"
registered_model_name = "<your_model_name>"
mlflow.register_model(model_uri=model_uri, name=registered_model_name)

 

Test the custom pyfunc model

To test the logged model in step above you can call the following code

## Code to test the model logged as custom pyfunc model which can be registered and deployed for serving
logged_model = 'runs:/run.info.run_id/model' #run from the pyfunc model logging
dataset = {"inputs": {"INFERENCE_TABLE_NAME" : "INFERENCE_BIKE_DATA_TBL"}}
loaded_model = mlflow.pyfunc.load_model(logged_model)
loaded_model.predict(dataset["inputs"])

Additionally the model endpoint can also be tested by using package uv with code below

run_id = run.info.run_id #run from the pyfunc model logging
model_uri = f"runs:/{run_id}/model"
dataset = {"inputs": {"INFERENCE_TABLE_NAME" : "INFERENCE_BIKE_DATA_TBL"}}
input_data = dataset
mlflow.models.predict(
    model_uri=model_uri,
    input_data=dataset["inputs"],
    env_manager="uv",
)

 

Create the Serving Endpoint

Now we have a registered model that can be deployed for serving. To do this I show the steps here to do it via the Databricks Serving UI  via the serving endpoint, it can also be done with code via Rest API or sdks. Go to Serving and Create a new Serving endpoint. Choose the registered_model_name from step above and add the environment variables for the HANA Cloud connection so the serving code can connect to HANA and call the model inference on user provided table name

Creating Serving Enpoint

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

HANA credentials as environment variables for deployment

 

Test the Serving Endpoint

The deployment as usual takes some minutes. Once the serving endpoint is in ready state, it can be tested with the usual ways when pressing Use 

Here is a sample screenshot for testing it in the Browser

Test Serving

Here is the corresponding code to test via Python

import os,json,requests
os.environ['DATABRICKS_TOKEN'] = "<Developer_Token>" #Token obtained by following https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/dev-tools/auth/pat

def score_model(dataset):
    url = '<serving_url>'
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {os.environ.get("DATABRICKS_TOKEN")}', 'Content-Type': 'application/json'}
    data_json = json.dumps(dataset, allow_nan=True)
    response = requests.request(method='POST', headers=headers, url=url, data=data_json)
    if response.status_code != 200:
        raise Exception(f'Request failed with status {response.status_code}, {response.text}')
    return response.json()

dataset = {'inputs': {'INFERENCE_TABLE_NAME' : "<hana_cloud_table_name_for_inference>"}}
res = score_model(dataset)
print(res)

 

Endpoint Consumption

The serving endpoint created above can be used in applications via rest API calls. In production the API would need to be secured via OAuth authenication . Here is a blogpost from @Ian_Henry  describing how such an endpoint can be triggered from SAP Analytics Cloud for example.

Conclusion

In this blogpost we show the power combination of using SAP HANA Cloud and model experiment tracking & serving capabilities from SAP Databricks via managed mlflow. This is suitable for usecases where data already resides in the HANA layer and the performance benefits from running hana-ml in data accessible via HANA Cloud in-memory is desirable while benefitting from model development support provided by SAP Databricks. 

The code for the above is available here on SAP-samples/hana-ml-samples.
